# anneal

> AI-powered Java migration assistant. Analyzes a Java codebase, detects version-specific
> risks and breaking changes (Java 8 → 25), and produces an incremental migration plan with
> code-level fix suggestions grounded in static analysis — not generic advice.
>
> Co-pilot, not autopilot. The tool surfaces, explains, and suggests. The developer decides.

## Project

- Docs: https://github.com/cloudrishi/anneal/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md
- Repo: https://github.com/cloudrishi/anneal

## Architecture

anneal is a multi-module Gradle (Kotlin DSL) project running on Java 25 / Quarkus 3.33.1.
Detection is fully deterministic — AST traversal via JavaParser, no LLM involvement.
LLM enrichment adds prose explanations to findings after detection completes.

```
anneal/
├── anneal-core/    # Domain model, rule engine, scanner — pure Java, no CDI
├── anneal-api/     # Quarkus REST layer — 4 endpoints, RuleRegistry, ScanResource
├── anneal-llm/     # LangChain4j enrichment — Ollama + Anthropic, ONNX embeddings
├── anneal-store/   # Panache entities, Flyway migrations V1–V5, pgvector
└── anneal-ui/      # Next.js 15 frontend — brutalist dark, IBM Plex Mono, molten orange
```

## Key design decisions

- Detection is LLM-free. AST traversal and rule matching are deterministic; no hallucinations.
- LLM enriches fix explanations only. Version facts from static analysis are injected as hard
  constraints into prompts to prevent model hallucination of Java version numbers.
- Local-first. Default provider is Ollama (codellama:13b for BREAKING findings, llama3.1:8b
  for prose). Anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-6) is opt-in via `allow-cloud-fallback: true`.
- Code never leaves the machine unless `allow-cloud-fallback: true` is explicitly set.
- Rules are Java classes, not database rows — testable, version-controlled, PR-reviewable.
- `autoApplicable: false` findings are never applied without developer approval.
- `llmExplanation` is not persisted — runtime enrichment only; history returns null honestly.

## Migration path

```
Java 8 → Java 11 (LTS) → Java 17 (LTS) → Java 21 (LTS) → Java 25 (LTS)
```

Java 9 is skipped as an intermediate stop (not LTS) but its risks (JPMS) are detected.

## Rule engine

22 rules across 6 categories covering Java 8 → 25:

| Category    | Rules | Boundary            |
|-------------|-------|---------------------|
| JPMS        | 4     | 8 → 9               |
| API_REMOVAL | 5     | 9 → 11, 17 → 21     |
| DEPRECATION | 3     | 11 → 17, 21 → 25    |
| LANGUAGE    | 4     | 8 → 25 (modernize)  |
| CONCURRENCY | 3     | 21 → 25 (modernize) |
| BUILD       | 3     | All boundaries      |

Detection patterns: AST_NODE, IMPORT, API_CALL, ANNOTATION, REFLECTION, BUILD.

## Data model (core types)

**Finding** — one per detected issue: `findingId`, `ruleId`, `severity` (BREAKING /
DEPRECATED / MODERNIZATION), `filePath`, `lineNumber`, `originalCode`, `confidence`,
`referenceUrl`, `status` (OPEN / ACCEPTED / REJECTED / DEFERRED).

**MigrationRule** — `ruleId`, `category`, `severity`, `effort` (TRIVIAL / LOW / MEDIUM /
HIGH / MANUAL), `introducedIn` (JavaVersion), `removedIn` (JavaVersion, nullable),
`patterns`, `fixTemplate` (FixSuggestion), `referenceUrl`.

**ScanResult** — `scanId`, `repoPath`, `detectedVersion`, `targetVersion` (always V25),
`findings`, `riskScore` (0–100), `phase` (ANALYSIS / PLANNING / FIXING / VALIDATION).

**EnrichedFix** — runtime only, not persisted: `findingId`, `explanation`, `modelUsed`.

## REST API

| Method | Path                 | Description                                    |
|--------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| GET    | /api/health          | Liveness                                       |
| POST   | /api/scan            | Scan repo — returns findings + LLM explanation |
| GET    | /api/scans           | List past scans (summary, no findings)         |
| GET    | /api/scans/{scanId}  | Full scan — llmExplanation is null on history  |

POST /api/scan flow: version detection → rule filtering → AST scan → persist →
LLM enrichment (failure-isolated per finding) → ONNX embed → pgvector store → response.

## LLM layer

Model routing:
- MANUAL effort + cloud enabled → claude-sonnet-4-6 (deep reasoning)
- BREAKING severity             → codellama:13b (JVM/API internals)
- DEPRECATED / MODERNIZATION   → llama3.1:8b (natural language prose)

Key implementation notes:
- Uses `ChatModel.chat(SystemMessage, UserMessage)` directly — not AiService proxies —
  because system messages are per-finding, not per-session.
- `FixPrompts.clean()` strips codellama:13b instruction-tuning artefacts ([INST], <<SYS>>).
- MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings (384-dim, ONNX, no network) per finding for similarity search.
- `EmbeddingRepository.findSimilar()` uses pgvector cosine operator (`<=>`) across past scans.

## Tech stack

| Layer          | Technology                           | Version  |
|----------------|--------------------------------------|----------|
| Backend        | Quarkus LTS                          | 3.33.1   |
| AST            | JavaParser                           | 3.28.0   |
| LLM            | LangChain4j                          | 1.13.0   |
| Embeddings     | langchain4j-embeddings-all-minilm-l6 | 0.36.2   |
| Vector store   | quarkus-langchain4j-pgvector         | 1.8.4    |
| Database       | PostgreSQL + pgvector                | pg16     |
| Frontend       | Next.js + TypeScript                 | 15 / 5   |
| Build          | Gradle Kotlin DSL                    | 9.4.1    |
| JDK            | Temurin                              | 25.0.2   |
| Lombok         | Lombok                               | 1.18.42  |

## Known dependency constraints (learned during implementation)

- `langchain4j-embeddings-all-minilm-l6-v2` last released at 0.36.2 — not updated to 1.13.0.
- `quarkus-langchain4j-bom` must NOT be declared alongside `langchain4j-bom` — two
  `enforcedPlatform` with `strictly` constraints deadlock Gradle resolution.
- `langchain4j-http-client-jdk` must be globally excluded — conflicts with Quarkiverse
  JAX-RS HTTP client at runtime.
- `ChatModel` (not `ChatLanguageModel`) and `.chatModel()` (not `.chatLanguageModel()`) —
  renamed in LangChain4j 1.0 GA.
- Lombok 1.18.40+ required for Java 25 support.
- `io.quarkus:quarkus-junit` (not `quarkus-junit5`) — renamed in Quarkus 3.31.

## Testing

38 tests, all passing in CI (GitHub Actions):
- `RiskScoreCalculatorTest` — 14 unit tests, includes score-66 regression anchor
- `RuleEngineTest` — 9 unit tests (AST, IMPORT, API_CALL, ANNOTATION, REFLECTION)
- `VersionDetectorTest` — 9 unit tests (Maven, Gradle, edge cases)
- `ScanResourceTest` — 6 integration tests against real pgvector/pgvector:pg16

CI: test job (pgvector service + Java 25 Temurin) → build job.
Cloud model validation: `CloudModelValidationIT` — opt-in, requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.

## Configuration

```yaml
anneal:
  llm:
    provider: ollama
    ollama:
      base-url: http://localhost:11434
      fix-model: codellama:13b
      prose-model: llama3.1:8b
    anthropic:
      api-key: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:}
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    allow-cloud-fallback: false   # code never leaves machine without this
    timeout-seconds: 120
    enrichment-enabled: true
```
